title: PR-1 projection protocol and continuous Simulation Worker session
summary: Implement ADR-0017's minimum schema-v3 GameSession projection contract as the sole sim-protocol writer, then host the PR-1 GameSessionRuntime with continuous fixed-30-TPS scheduling, fail-closed validation, backpressure and Worker/headless parity.
acceptance: The existing Worker message families and protocolVersion 1 are retained, SIM_SCHEMA_VERSION is bumped from 2 to 3, and GAME_SESSION_PROJECTION_VERSION 1 is exported from the sim-protocol public root exactly as approved by ADR-0017

InitSession can explicitly request { kind: game_session, version: 1 }, Ready echoes the accepted contract, and negotiated RenderSnapshot.gameSession plus UiDelta.gameSession payloads carry one coherent tick/snapshot/world/content/map/version basis

GameSessionRenderProjectionV1 represents stable entity refs, resident/resource/structure/lamp/build-site render kinds, render def ids, authoritative Q16 positions, facing, animation and flags needed for presentation interpolation

GameSessionUiProjectionV1 represents pause/speed, day index/tick/phase/daylight, at least eight resident summaries, food/wood/stone/lamp-oil quantities, job markers, structured alerts and requested resident/resource/structure selection detail without localized authority prose

Protocol/runtime tests reject schema-v2 peers, unknown projection versions, mismatched Ready contracts, missing negotiated payloads, malformed rows and incoherent basis; the browser session enters a structured fatal/closed state and never falls back to static fixtures, while lower stale render sequences remain droppable

The only apps/web change permitted by WM-0164 is apps/web/src/simulation-worker-session.test.ts: update consumer-side assertions and fixtures from schema 2 to schema 3 and, where negotiation is exercised, use a GameSession-compatible negotiated fixture; apps/web production source, product routing and UI behavior remain forbidden
Legacy M0-M8 scenario modes may omit the projection request for same-build regression use under schema 3 but are mechanically ineligible for the default PR-1 product route

Simulation Worker initializes and hosts the PR-1 GameSessionRuntime from WM-0163 through InitSession/LoadSession/PlayerCommandBatch/SetSpeed/Pause/RequestUiDetail/RequestSave/Shutdown without adding a message family
Worker owns normal product scheduling: fixed 30 TPS at speed 1, 60 at speed 2, 90 at speed 3, and 0 when paused or speed 0; sim-core receives only integer tick advancement
Pause preserves requested speed while stopping automatic ticks, SetSpeed affects future scheduling only, and command results remain reliable around pause/speed changes
RenderSnapshot/UiDelta publication is latest-wins under backpressure, while CommandResult, SaveReady, FatalSimulationError and structured alerts are not dropped
WM-0160 explicit advance/drain helpers remain available for tests/tools but are not used as the default gameplay clock
Worker and Node headless produce matching checkpoint world/read-model hashes for the same seed, scenario definition and fixed command stream
RequestSave/LoadSession for GameSession either use an explicitly versioned focused PR-1 snapshot or fail closed with structured unsupported reasons; no public save compatibility promise is added
Browser Worker smoke proves real Chromium Worker scheduling emits advancing PR-1 GameSession projections without UI dispatching advance helper commands as normal time
No apps/web product UI, renderer-pixi, ui-react, Electron preload, save format, new runtime dependency or release action is changed
The report records schema/projection compatibility evidence, scheduler policy, backpressure behavior, reliable-message evidence, parity hashes and any residual PR-1 blockers

history.details: Scoped as the Worker host/scheduler task after WM-0163; remains proposed and must not introduce protocol/schema/save drift without a new gate.

Expanded under ADR-0017 to be the sole schema-v3 GameSession projection protocol writer plus continuous Worker scheduler; assigned exact sim-protocol files/tests and a serial edge to WM-0165. Task remains proposed and unclaimed.

WM-0164 requires SIM_SCHEMA_VERSION=3 and schema-v2 fail-closed plus pnpm quality, but apps/web/src/simulation-worker-session.test.ts still hardcodes/emits schemaVersion 2. Full quality fails 3 tests (expected schema 2 and two helper timeouts after schema-2 messages are correctly rejected). That file is outside WM-0164 allowedPaths and ADR-0017 forbids a schema-2 compatibility workaround. Add the focused apps/web test path to allowedPaths or land a separate schema-3 consumer-test update, then unblock.

Resolved MSG-MREYCOIN-DC0D8D by adding only apps/web/src/simulation-worker-session.test.ts to allowedPaths and required focused checks. That test may update schema-2 consumer assertions/fixtures to schema 3 and a negotiated GameSession-compatible fixture only; apps/web production source, product routing and UI remain forbidden. ADR-0017 schema-v2 fail-closed behavior remains unchanged.

Implemented schema-v3 GameSession projection v1 and public validation, continuous 0/30/60/90 TPS Simulation Worker hosting, latest-wins projection outbox with reliable messages, fail-closed browser negotiation, Chromium autonomous scheduling smoke, Worker/headless parity, schema-3 Web consumer fixtures, and documented save/load limitations; all WM-0164 required checks and 445-test quality gate pass

changes_requested: High: packages/sim-worker/src/game-session-worker-host.ts:111-137 mutates the authoritative command queue before validating the whole batch; a [Noop, PrioritizeLampWork] batch returns batchAccepted=false at packages/sim-worker/src/index.ts:403-409 but the leaked Noop changes the next checkpoint hashes (control 0x0fba7fbb/0x7c1484a3 versus rejected mixed batch 0x1e21d503/0xd4ad21ee). Preflight all commands or provide per-command results without reporting a rejected batch that already mutated authority. High: packages/sim-protocol/src/game-session-projection-validation.ts:199-201,360-385 validates selectionDetail.basis only structurally and never requires its snapshotSequence to equal the containing UI basis; a projection at snapshot 2 with selection snapshot 0 returns {ok:true}, so stale/foreign requested detail is accepted as coherent. Medium: packages/sim-worker/src/game-session-browser-validation.ts:58-81 accepts a second matching Ready after activation; repro remains active, emits two Ready messages and does not terminate. Medium: packages/sim-protocol/src/game-session-projection-validation.ts:105-113 returns InvalidPayload for projection version 99, contrary to ADR-0017:210-211 requiring UnsupportedSchemaVersion. Medium: packages/sim-worker/src/index.ts:253-280,496-498 leaves the 100ms interval live after Shutdown until disconnect; state guards prevent extra ticks, but the dedicated Worker continues callback wakeups/resource retention. Add focused regressions for each. All WM-0164 required checks passed (protocol 16, worker/core 24, Web 7, Chromium 18, 18k, boundaries/handoff/taskctl/diff, quality 77 files/445 tests), confirming coverage gaps rather than incidental gate failures.

Addressed MSG-MRF0IYDJ-4855D8: atomic PlayerCommandBatch preflight preserves control hashes, selection detail carries and validates the full UI basis, duplicate Ready fails closed, projection version 99 returns UnsupportedSchemaVersion, accepted Shutdown clears the scheduler immediately with callback/disconnect safety, and oversized validator/browser/test files are split below 400 lines; all focused, Chromium, 18k, boundary, handoff, taskctl, diff, and 449-test quality gates pass

verified: Re-review verified fix 04c1416. Direct repros confirm rejected [Noop, unsupported] batches are atomic with control-identical hashes 0x0fba7fbb/0x7c1484a3; every selection-detail projection basis field mismatch fails; duplicate Ready produces fatal and one terminate; version 99 returns UnsupportedSchemaVersion; accepted Shutdown clears the interval immediately, queued callbacks add no posts/ticks, and repeated disconnect is idempotent. Preflight accepts empty and exactly 128 append-only commands, rejects 129 atomically with control hashes, and preserves batchAccepted semantics. Selection basis extends the complete schema-v3 projection-v1 basis without protocolVersion/message-family drift. Related GameSession files are all below 400 lines, external imports use package roots, and boundary checks find no cycle/deep import. Required protocol 17, worker/core 22, Web 7, Chromium 18, 18k, typecheck, boundaries, handoff, taskctl, diff checks, extra browser 5, and full quality 78 files/449 tests all pass. No blocking findings remain.

protocolFileOwnership.schemaChange: SIM_SCHEMA_VERSION 2 -> 3; GAME_SESSION_PROJECTION_VERSION = 1; no new message family

saveLoadStance: No public save compatibility is introduced. RequestSave/LoadSession behavior for GameSession must be versioned focused evidence or fail-closed unsupported behavior.
forbiddenPaths: packages/sim-protocol/** outside the exact allowedPaths list; WM-0164 is the only protocol writer in the PR-1 DAG
new Worker message family, projection version other than GameSession v1, command-contract change or schema change beyond ADR-0017 schema 3 without a new reviewed ADR

apps/** except the exact apps/web/src/simulation-worker-session.test.ts consumer-test path; no apps/web production source, product route, UI behavior, renderer-pixi/ui-react or Electron preload changes
sim-core runtime ownership changes beyond consuming WM-0163 public root exports
using WM-0160 drain/advance helpers as the default gameplay clock
hidden global Worker/session singleton, timer authority inside sim-core, dropped reliable messages or UI-owned simulation state
public save compatibility, migration, release, telemetry, accounts, paid services or public feedback surfaces

review.summary: Re-review verified fix 04c1416. Direct repros confirm rejected [Noop, unsupported] batches are atomic with control-identical hashes 0x0fba7fbb/0x7c1484a3; every selection-detail projection basis field mismatch fails; duplicate Ready produces fatal and one terminate; version 99 returns UnsupportedSchemaVersion; accepted Shutdown clears the interval immediately, queued callbacks add no posts/ticks, and repeated disconnect is idempotent. Preflight accepts empty and exactly 128 append-only commands, rejects 129 atomically with control hashes, and preserves batchAccepted semantics. Selection basis extends the complete schema-v3 projection-v1 basis without protocolVersion/message-family drift. Related GameSession files are all below 400 lines, external imports use package roots, and boundary checks find no cycle/deep import. Required protocol 17, worker/core 22, Web 7, Chromium 18, 18k, typecheck, boundaries, handoff, taskctl, diff checks, extra browser 5, and full quality 78 files/449 tests all pass. No blocking findings remain.
